summary: Tested bulk student import feature end-to-end. All backend API tests (6/6) and frontend UI tests passed. Import modal, CSV validation, preview, error handling, and successful import all working correctly.

test_results.preview_table: ✅ Valid CSV shows preview table with 2 rows
test_results.invalid_csv_error: ✅ Invalid CSV (missing columns) shows error: 'CSV missing required columns: first_name, last_name, year_level, class_name, teacher'
test_results.confirm_btn_enabled_after_csv: ✅ confirm-import-btn disabled=False after valid CSV loaded
test_results.backend_import_valid: ✅ POST /api/students/import with valid data imports 2 students correctly
test_results.backend_import_missing_fields: ✅ Import with missing required fields returns error rows (1 imported, 2 errors out of 3)
test_results.persistence: ✅ Imported students appear in GET /api/students list
test_results.students_count_updates: ✅ After import, student count updated from 36 to 37
test_results.import_success_feedback: ✅ '1 of 1 students imported successfully' message shown after import

critical_code_review_comments: The ImportModal passes `parsed.rows` which includes `_row` key (line index added by parseCSV). Backend receives this extra field but ignores it - no impact, but could be cleaned up for cleaner data
No duplicate import prevention - same CSV can be imported multiple times creating duplicate students
seed_data_creation: Created test session: test_session_import_1773449753565 (admin@import.tester). Imported test students via UI tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test session: test_session_import_1773449753565 (admin role). All import feature tests passing. Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_import_students.py.
